content: Autonomous agents can use the intrinsic reward signal from an ICM curiosity mechanism to proactively chunk and compress unprocessed information into working memory, offsetting the upper-bound reasoning limits imposed by fixed working memory capacity. This creates a self-regulating cycle where curiosity drives dynamic working memory expansion that scales with the reasoning demands of a task, rather than being capped by static hardware or architecture constraints.
source_topics: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
trigger_topic: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
